title: Dalbergia scortechinii
taxonomy: Dalbergia scortechinii is a species of plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to Malaysia and is often found in tropical forests. The classification of Dalbergia scortechinii is as a tree, belonging to the genus Dalbergia. The tree is further classified as a member of the subfamily Faboideae. It is known to be a hardwood tree with a wide range of applications.
uses: Dalbergia scortechinii, also known as the Borneo rosewood, is a valuable species with various medicinal uses. The plant's extracts have been traditionally used in Southeast Asian countries to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory diseases. Its bark and leaves are also known to possess antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat infections. Furthermore, the plant's wood is used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues and skin problems. Additionally, the extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
